title: wahlenbergia gracilis f. multicaulis
intro: Wahlenbergia gracilis f. multicaulis, commonly known as the New Zealand bluebell or creeping wahlenbergia, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Wahlenbergia gracilis f. multicaulis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.
